title: Playground Command Lens
concept: A command-palette-first redesign where search is the primary navigation model, grouped results expose every Playground action, and an inspector pane explains the selected workflow without hiding the active WordPress context.
focus: saved Playground management at launcher level
warm editorial accents with dense operational controls
flows: Search or filter commands to create a vanilla Playground, preview WordPress PRs, preview Gutenberg PRs or branches, import from GitHub, run a Blueprint URL, or import a zip.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with progress visible beside the command inspector.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds from the launcher, including opening, renaming, and deleting browser-backed entries.
Open Site Manager surfaces for settings, file browser, Blueprint editing, database tools, logs, and additional export actions.
Browse featured Blueprint shortcuts or the full 43-item Blueprint gallery with category filters.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
edit WordPress version, PHP version, language, network, older versions, and multisite settings
understand destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
use File browser with create folder, create file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
inspect Playground, WordPress, and PHP logs
